series_metadata.series_title: Denied Self Insured Identification Number (SIDN) Application File
series_metadata.series_description: The Denied Self Insured Identification Number (SIDN) Application File is an application that is denied self insurance, and the employer is given 14 days to appeal to the Self Insured Review Panel (SIRP). The Self Insured Identification Number (SIDN) Application File is scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS). The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.

retention_rules.disposition: Electronic: Retain for 1 year, and then destroy as confidential. ; Paper: Scan the original document into UDS, verify the scanned image is readable, and then destroy., Electronic: Delete ; Paper: Shred
